JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)
DECLARATION OF DANIEL PATTERSON IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 100063214) FILED BY DEFENDANT ALFRED'S LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALFRED'S LLC'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS AND DAMAGES AND TO STAY LITIGATION PENDING RESOLUTION CONTINUED PER PARTIES' REQUEST. DEFENDANTS' PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF ALL THE COMPLAINT'S COUNTS SAVE THE PRIVATE ATTORNEY GENERAL COUNT IS GRANTED AND THIS LITIGATION IS STAYED PENDING THE ARBITRATION. (CCP 1281.4.) THE TWO ARBITRATION AGREEMENTS' EXISTENCE IS UNDISPUTED. THE AGREEMENTS ARE NOT INVALID BECAUSE "PERMEATED WITH UNCONSCIONABILITY," AS PLAINTIFFS ARGUE. TWO SENTENCES IN THE THREE-PAGE SINGLE-SPACED AGREEMENTS - REGARDING "AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEE" DETERMINATION AND A JURY TRIAL WAIVER - ARE UNENFORCEABLE, BUT SEVERABLE PER THE AGREEMENTS' TERMS. (SERAFIN V. BALCO PROPERTIES LTD., LLC (2015) 235 CAL.APP.4TH 165, 183-184). LABOR CODE 229 IS PREEMPTED BY THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT, WHICH GOVERNS THESE ARBITRATION AGREEMENTS. (PERRY V. THOMAS (1987) 482 U.S. 483, 492 .) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

Defendant Alfred'S Llc'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Individual Claims And Damages And To Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Continued Per Parties' Request
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nina Kani, Esq., Kani Law Group, telephone 949-629-2484, appearing for Defendant Alfreds, LLC;
No other appearance.

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Alfred'S Llc'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Individual Claims And Damages And To Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Continued Per Parties' Request.

Defendants' petition to compel arbitration of all the complaint's counts save the Private Attorney General count is GRANTED and this litigation is STAYED pending the arbitration. (CCP 1281.4.) The two arbitration agreements' existence is undisputed. The agreements are not invalid because "permeated with unconscionability," as plaintiffs argue. Two sentences in the three-page single-spaced agreements - regarding "aggrieved employee" determination and a jury trial waiver - are unenforceable, but severable per the agreements' terms. (Serafin v. Balco Properties Ltd., LLC (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 165, 183-184). Labor Code 229 is preempted by the Federal Arbitration Act, which governs these arbitration agreements. (Perry v. Thomas (1987) 482 U.S. 483, 492 .)

order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

DEFENDANT ALFRED'S LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Individual Claims And Damages And To Stay Litigation Pending Resolution.

CONTINUED to February 22, 2019 at parties' request.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
